title: SoFi's $1.6 Billion EBITDA Target: The Path to Fintech Profitability
text: SoFi Technologies is targeting $1.6 billion in adjusted EBITDA for 2026, demonstrating a clear path to sustainable profitability. This target represents a 62% increase from Q1 2026's adjusted EBITDA of $340 million and reflects management's confidence in the business model. The company achieved its first quarter of GAAP profitability in Q4 2023 and has now reported nine consecutive quarters of GAAP profitability. In 2024, SoFi achieved its first full year of profitability with net income of $100 million. For 2026, the company guides to an adjusted EBITDA margin of approximately 30%, achieved through strong revenue growth and improving operational leverage. The path to profitability has been supported by the company's national bank charter, which enables SoFi to fund loans with its own deposit base and capture higher net interest margins. The company's membership base has grown to 14.7 million with 35% YoY growth, providing a large addressable market for cross-selling and fee-based revenue growth. SoFi's EBITDA expansion targets suggest the company is transitioning from a growth-focused fintech to a profitable, sustainable banking and fintech platform.
